entry.title: Migration Cockpit in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

Migration Cockpit

  • Explore Migration Cockpit
  • Using Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage
  • Transfer List
  • Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

Explore Migration Objects: A New SAP Fiori App for Enhanced Project Planning

A successful data migration relies on meticulous planning and a deep understanding of data dependencies. With the 2602 release, we are introducing the SAP Fiori app, "Explore Migration Objects," to bring a new level of clarity to your project preparation.

This app provides crucial insights into migration object dependencies and their relationships to specific scope items. It empowers your project team to efficiently plan data migration projects by ensuring that all mandatory and optional predecessor objects are correctly identified and migrated in the proper sequence. Through interactive graphs, you can now visualize how migration objects are interconnected. The app allows for detailed dependency analysis, differentiating between mandatory and optional predecessors, and provides focused search and direct access to migration object documentation. By visualizing these relationships, your team can make informed decisions, ultimately reducing project risks and optimizing timelines. Please note that this initial version of the app currently supports migration objects built for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach.

 

 

Enhanced Flexibility with Staging Tables for Direct System Migration

For customers using the "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System" approach, the 2602 release introduces a powerful new capability: the use of staging tables as intermediate storage. This feature is designed to facilitate the adjustment of selected data before it is loaded into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This enhancement significantly increases transparency and provides robust validation capabilities for your selected data. It gives your team more flexibility to adjust and cleanse application data from the source SAP system before the Now, data adaptation is performed within ETL tool prior to loading the data into the migration cockpit’s staging tables. Once the data is prepared and validated, you can transfer the ready-for-processing data to the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit for further processing, ensuring higher data quality in your new cloud environment.

 

 

 

Streamline Your Migration Troubleshooting with the New Transfer List

Transfer List

In any complex data migration project, ensuring data is transferred accurately is paramount. When issues arise during the simulation or migration phases, pinpointing the root cause can be time-consuming. To bring greater transparency to this process, we are introducing the Transfer List for the migration approach "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System." This feature is designed for data migration specialists and project teams who need deep visibility into the data transfer process for effective and efficient troubleshooting.


A Clear View for Faster Resolution

The Transfer List provides a detailed view of the data exactly as it is passed to the target API that creates the records in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
This capability is particularly beneficial when you need to troubleshoot the simulation or migration process. For example, your team can now analyze the Transfer List to definitively determine whether an issue lies with the target API itself or if it is related to a problem in the structure or field mapping. This clarity helps you isolate problems faster, reduce resolution time, and keep your migration project on track.

To use this feature, the simulation or migration process must first be completed for the relevant migration object instance. Once complete, you can access the Transfer List with the following steps:

1. From the Migration Project screen, select the "Instances" link for your object.
2. On the Migration Object Instances screen, select the instance you wish to investigate.
3. Choose the "Transfer List" button.

On the subsequent screen, you can view the target parameters, fields, and their corresponding values that were used in the transfer, providing your team with the precise information needed for analysis. This enhancement empowers you to resolve issues with confidence and precision, ensuring a smoother transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

To further streamline your data migration activities, we have increased the file size limits for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach. This practical update allows for more efficient handling of large data volumes.

When you upload your data using XML files, you can bundle multiple files into a single zip file for a more convenient upload. With this release, the maximum size for each individual XML file contained within the zip file has been increased to 400MB. This allows you to process larger data sets in a single file. Please be aware that while individual files can be larger, the total size of the zip file itself should not exceed 100MB upon upload to the migration cockpit. This improvement simplifies the data loading process and reduces the number of uploads required for extensive data sets.

I hope you're as excited about these updates as I am! To learn more, I encourage you to explore the SAP Help Portal and join the conversation on the SAP Community.

What is New?

Check Migration Cockpit Community

SAP Help Data Migration

SAP Community YouTube presented by Claudia Gemm and Elizaveta Kozhevenikova

  

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Migration Cockpit as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: Usage of Product Hierarchy in S4 Margin Analysis
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

we have the requirement to manage a Product Hierarchy (App Manage Global Hierarchies) which should be used in S4 Margin Analysis.

It seems, that the Product Hierarchy cannot be selected and used in Apps for Margin Analysis, ie. Line Items Margin Analysis.

We tried to create a CDS-view to combine the GL line item (I_GLAccountLineItem) CDS view with the product hierarchy CDS view (_I_ProdUnivHierarchyNodeBasic), but this seems to be not possible, because following error message for the end date of the hierarchy is raised: Field PRODHIERARCHYVALIDITYENDDATE in view YY9_A43EF4A2F441542AE73336 has date semantics. Cannot be used in association _PRODUCTHIERARCHY.

Is there an option in S4 to use the Product Hierarchy with S4 Margin Analysis?

Thanks for your support.

entry.title: UI5 MessageStrip - Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
entry.content:
Hi, 

I discovered this in a UI5 webapp (not using Fiori Elements) while developing. 

Component: sap.m.MessageStrip
Version affected: 1.143.0+ (until current, 1.144.1)
Version working: 1.142.6
Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
Reproduction: <MessageStrip class="sapUiSmallMargin" text="Test"/>

Even removing the class attribute triggers this failure. There is no MessageStrip in the code, that has more than one CSS class element. There is furthermore no usage of addStyleClass() etc. in the controller logic.

entry.title: Availability Control Issues in budget profile with actitity groups ++
entry.content:
We have created budget profiles with activity group ++ with Action 3 and usage 100% for our wbs projects. These budget profiles have only annual values activated in order to update and maintain budget year by year.

The intention was to trigger availability control if a PR/PO/SES/MIGO/MIRO exceed the approved budget during the year.


What we are facing is that SAP allows to create SES/MIGO  with  cases from previous years (commitments)  for WBS elements that do not have any approved budget for the current year, and post actuals.

We do not use carry forward transactions (budget//commitments) as a business policy. 

Is there any case that the ++ group transaction is not sufficient for our case and we should register additionally in the budget profile another transaction entry?

The logic in PTP is indeed different, as the interface is more standardized and embedded directly within the ABAP code.

If you would like to understand how fields are mapped, I recommend reviewing the ABAP class CL_HRSFEC_EE_MDR_MAIN as a starting point.

Best Regards

Giorgio

entry.title: When to Use SAP Central Invoice Management (CIM) vs. SAP S/4HANA Convergent Invoicing
entry.content:
Introduction

Organizations often struggle with managing invoices efficiently—whether it’s supplier invoices in procurement or customer invoices in complex billing scenarios. SAP offers two powerful solutions: Central Invoice Management (CIM) and Convergent Invoicing (CI) in S/4HANA. While both deal with invoices, they serve very different business needs.

This blog explores real-world business stories to help you decide when to use each.

 

Business Story 1: Global Procurement and Vendor Invoice Chaos

Scenario:
A multinational company receives thousands of supplier invoices daily in multiple formats—PDFs, scanned documents, and even paper. Manual entry leads to delays, compliance risks, and missed early payment discounts.

Solution:
SAP Central Invoice Management (CIM) is the hero here.

Key Features of CIM:

  • Central capture of invoices via OCR and document extraction
  • Intelligent AI/ML-driven classification and matching to purchase orders
  • Fiori-based apps for end-to-end tracking, status monitoring, and approvals
  • Role-based access control and audit-ready workflows
  • Built on SAP BTP for extensibility and seamless cloud integration

End to end process flow

  1. Invoice Capture
  • Supplier sends invoice via email, portal, or paper
  • CIM uses OCR and AI/ML to extract data and classify the invoice 

2.Invoice Validation & Matching

  • CIM matches invoice against Purchase Order (PO) and Goods Receipt.
  • Discrepancies flagged for approval workflow.

3.Approval Workflow

  • Fiori-based apps route invoice to approvers.
  • Role-based access ensures compliance and audit readiness.

4.Posting to S/4HANA

  • Approved invoice posted in S/4HANA as an AP document:
    • Debit: Expense or Inventory
    • Credit: Vendor Payables

5.Payment Execution

  • Standard Payment Run (F110) in S/4HANA:
    • Debit: Vendor Payables
    • Credit: Bank Clearing Account

6.Reporting & Monitoring

  • CIM dashboard tracks invoice status, approvals, and payment completion.

 

 Business Story 2: Telecom Billing Complexity

Scenario:

A telecom provider offers subscriptions, usage-based services, and partner settlements. Customers expect a single consolidated invoice, but billing data comes from multiple systems and services. So the goal is to consolidate multiple billing items into one customer invoice.

Solution:

SAP S/4HANA Convergent Invoicing (CI) steps in.

Key Features of CI:

  • Aggregates diverse billing events (usage, subscriptions, services) into one invoice
  • Uses FI‑CA (Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable) as the financial backbone
  • Offers rating, billing, and invoicing capabilities for high-volume, consumption-based services
  • Available via both classic SAP menu and modern Fiori applications

End to End process flow

1.Billing Data Collection

  • Usage records, subscription fees, and partner charges collected from multiple systems.

2.Rating & Charging

  • BRIM component calculates charges based on usage and pricing rules.

3.Billing Document Creation

  • CI aggregates all charges into a single billing document.

4.Invoice Generation

  • Invoice created in S/4HANA:
  • Debit: Customer Receivables
  • Credit: Revenue Accounts

5.Revenue Recognition

  • Revenue recognized based on IFRS/GAAP rules (e.g., at service delivery or billing).

6.Payment Collection

  • Customer pays via standard AR processes:
  • Debit: Bank Account
  • Credit: Customer Receivables

7.Reporting & Analytics

  • Fiori apps provide billing and revenue insights.
Summary
Key Differences at a Glance
AspectCentral Invoice managementConvergent Invoicing
Focus
Supplier/Vendor invoices (AP)
Customer invoices (AR)
Domain
Procure-to-Pay
Order-to-Cash
Technology
SAP BTP, OCR, AI
S/4HANA BRIM, FI-CA
Use case
Invoice capture & approval
 Billing consolidation

entry.title: Re: Business Partner creation $batch fails at “Call Web S...
entry.content:
Hi @moon14 ,

The HEAD request succeeds, so your credentials are valid. The 401 on POST typically points to a CSRF token issue - verify the x-csrf-token from the HEAD response is being passed in the POST request headers.

Your Postman test idea is a good next step. If the same Communication User works in Postman with the full HEAD -> POST flow, that narrows it down to something BPA-specific in the template configuration.

entry.title: Re: How to Map Fiori Apps to Their Underlying Data Source...
entry.content:
Dear @Minsung_Lee,

In Public Cloud (Fiori-only) you can build a pretty solid lineage picture but it won’t be 100% complete for every standard app because SAP intentionally limits visibility into non-released backend artifacts and some apps don’t rely purely on CDS tables in a straightforward way. The best results come from combining Fiori Apps Reference Library, App runtime inspection and View/Query Browser.

Below is a practical, repeatable approach:

Point 1.  App ->OData service

Option A: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library best starting point

For a given app by App ID / name open the app details and check Implementation Information where SAP lists the OData service(s) used by the app and often other technical artifacts

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F3191')/S36

 

Best practice: In the Apps Library List View you can add columns like OData Services and export useful if you want a catalog-wide mapping

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/fiori-for-sap-s-4hana-identifying-the-odata-services-cds-views-and/ba-p/13411123

 

Option B: Inside Public Cloud the About

Many apps expose technical details via the user menu About. Community guidance commonly points to these as the first in-system checks for service names

More details below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/finding-odata-service-s-for-a-fiori-aap/qaq-p/14160715

Option C: Browser developer tools, works even with no backend GUI access

Open the app -> browser DevTools ->Network and filter for:

  • /sap/opu/odata/sap/ (OData V2)
  • /sap/opu/odata4/sap/ (OData V4)

You’ll typically see a call to …/$metadata or requests whose URL contains the service technical name. This is the most reliable way to confirm what the app actually calls at runtime apps often call multiple services: main + value helps + attachments etc.

Point 2. OData service ->CDS view

Once you have the service root, open the metadata:

  • V2: …/sap/opu/odata/sap/<SERVICE>/$metadata
  • V4: …/sap/opu/odata4/sap/<SERVICE>/…/$metadata

From $metadata, note the EntitySet / EntityType names. For many apps the service is generated from CDS and you’ll see strong naming hints:

  • services often end with _CDS
  • entity sets often match or closely resemble the CDS consumption view name

Then jump to View Browser app and search those view names

Point 3. CDS view

Use View Browser (F2170) because is designed to explore CDS views content, annotations and underlying artifacts without needing backend transactions

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2170')/S35

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/0bde695751505c08e10000000a441470.html

What to look for in View Browser:

  • View type / layer (Consumption / Query / Cube / Interface etc.)
  • Cross reference / data sources (which views/tables are referenced)
  • Release status (released for key user / cloud development vs not released)

Important: You will not always get a full down to table level chain for SAP standard content:

  • If a CDS view is not released, details may be restricted even if you can see its name
  • Some standard artifacts may be missing in View Browser but visible elsewhere, SAP notes this happens in certain cases

So yes View Browser is effective but your mileage depends on what SAP has released for your use case.

SAP Notes and SAP Community below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3504675

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/how-to-find-cds-views-in-s-4hana-public-cloud-edition/ba-p/14181383

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3439701

Point 4. Query Browser

Query Browser is used to discover and run analytical queries, which are CDS views marked as analytical queries

Practical steps:

  1. In Query Browser (F1068) find your query and note its technical name this is typically the CDS query view name
  2. Use the navigation from Query Browser or manually to open the same object in View Browser

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1068')/S36

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/3a24b854ee8f8d21e10000000a44176d.html

Summary:

  1. Start in Fiori Apps Reference Library to capture apps OData service(s)
  2. Verify at runtime in DevTools Network confirm real calls capture $metadata
  3. From $metadata, list main entity sets map names to CDS views
  4. In View Browser, open each CDS view and follow Cross reference/data sources recursively until you reach tables where allowed
  5. For analytics start from Query Browser

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Impact of Not Settling a Maintenance Order in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I would like to ask for your guidance regarding Maintenance Orders in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

If costs or expenses have been posted to a Maintenance Order, but the order is not settled, what would be the impact on the system?

Specifically, if the order is processed up to Technical Completion (TECO), but Settlement and Business Completion (CLSD) are not performed, could this cause any issues or limitations?
For example:

  • Impact on Month-End or Year-End Closing

  • Open balances remaining on the Maintenance Order

  • Effects on Financial Reporting or Controlling

  • Any system checks or errors during period-end activities

I would appreciate any insights or best practices on whether settlement is mandatory before closing periods, and what potential risks may occur if settlement is not completed.

Thank you very much in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Jessada

entry.title: Re: How to get insights in the data footprint of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant
entry.content:
Hi @shaji_narayanan ,

Q: "Does the total GB value given in the Cloud ALM metric/card/tile "Top Tables: Table Sizes" shows the total used disk space at the OS level?" 

A: No, it shows the memory size of the tables aggregated by application component. And there is indeed no place where the disk space usage is exposed. Neither in Cloud ALM nor in the Technical Monitoring apps that are accessed locally at the respective S/4 tenant.
The reason for exposing HANA memory related figures only is, that this is the infrastructure part, that SAP customers should care about as this impacts the license consumption, whereas HANA disk usage has no impact ... and is anyway fully managed by SAP's cloud operations.

Q: "You also mentioned C414 (DB size license measurement?); where I can view the same and current vs historical usage stats?"

A: The size of a table on memory and HANA disk can differ as not necessarily and not always ALL data is loaded from HANA disk to HANA memory. The C414 metric works on the assumption that all data is loaded to memory as this provides the most stable and least volatile numbers. The later license metrics (CY263 and CY500) are then based on the actual size of a table in HANA memory.
You can get the view on historical C414 numbers in the license dashboard in SAP for Me.

entry.title: Agrupamento de itens MRP na ordem via MD01
entry.content:
Gostaria de saber se é possível o agrupamento de itens MRP nas ordens planejadas geradas pela MD01?

Pelo que vi é gerada uma ordem para cada item e consequentemente uma requisição de compra para cada ordem. Gostaria de agrupar estes itens em uma ordem ou saber se é possível agrupar várias ordens planejada para uma requisição via MD15? 

Exemplo tenho um grupo de mercadoria com 10 itens de MRP, ao gerar as ordens planejadas geraria 1 ordem com estes 10 itens e 1 Requisição com estes 10 itens.

Caso alguém tenha informações ou indicações de que caminhos seguir, agradeço!!

entry.title: Title: Facing issues deploying Java Spring Boot app with MySQL to SAP BTP – need guidance
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I’m new to SAP BTP and currently struggling with deploying my local Java application. I hope someone can guide me in the right direction.

I have built a Spring Boot application where:

Backend is Java + Spring Boot

Frontend is HTML, CSS, JavaScript (served from the same Spring Boot app)

Database is MySQL

It’s a single monolithic app (UI + APIs in one JAR)

What works locally

Application runs fine on my local machine

UI loads correctly

APIs work

MySQL connection works locally

What I tried on SAP BTP

Built the app using mvn clean package

Installed Cloud Foundry CLI

Logged in to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry

Tried deploying using cf push

Problems I am facing

Application fails or behaves unexpectedly after deployment

MySQL connection does not work properly from BTP

Confusion about database setup (external MySQL vs SAP HANA)

Unsure if I need to use CAP / CDS or if plain Spring Boot is supported

Not clear how to properly configure datasource properties using environment variables

My main questions

Can a normal Spring Boot application (without CAP/CDS) be deployed to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry?

Is it okay to use external MySQL with SAP BTP, or should I migrate to SAP HANA?

What is the correct way to configure database credentials in BTP?

Are there any mandatory BTP services I must bind for a basic Spring Boot app?

Any recommended best practices for full-stack Spring Boot apps on BTP?

My goal is to successfully deploy my application and access it through the Cloud Foundry URL provided by BTP.

Any help, examples, or pointers to the right documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Application Process Task List (Planner) – Business Ca...
entry.content:
Good day @sandeep_shankarampet 

Thank you for your question.

What you described (missing SAP_EAM_BC_TL_MC catalog) seems like you might be missing the content delivery rather than role assignment only.

I suggest you check your CBC scope activation for Asset Management, then use Maintain Business Roles to verify delivered EAM role templates.

 

Here is a quick guide:

In your CBC, confirm Asset Management scope items for task list planner or active in your project. If this is missing, the catalog will not be delivered.

Then open the Maintain Business Roles app to check your business role templates. Search for EAM planner roles and check if they contain task list apps. Assign the delivered role template to yourself, then you may also assign others.

Open Business Catalogs app, search for SAP_EAM_BC_TL_MC, you may also search by description EAM – Task list. If you still do not have it, then it is not delivered in your current tenant content.

If the catalog still does not exist in Business Catalogs app, then raise an incident with your tenant release, etc.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: How to Add a Field to a Migration Object in SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)
entry.content:
Migrating data to SAP S/4HANA Cloud sometimes requires adapting standard migration objects to fit your business needs. You might need to add a field to the XML or CSV template files for the SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit that isn’t included by default.  The App "Model your migration" (SAP S/4HANA Migration Object Modeler) makes this possible—here’s a step-by-step guide using the supplier object as an example.

❗This blog is only relevant, if you use the approach "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables".

Prerequisites

  • Access to the SAP Fiori app “Model Your Migration Objects”
  • The required role: SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR
  • You are using the approach "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables"
  • Check that the field you want to add is released for use (see the relevant SAP Note, e.g., SAP Note 3019787 for Supplier). In this example we will add field CONTACTALLOWANCECTRCENBP to the migration object Supplier.
  • Collective Note for all supported migration objects SAP Note 2999428

Step-by-Step Guide: Adding a New Field to the Supplier Migration Object

Example: add field CONTACTALLOWANCECTRCENBP to the migration object Supplier.

1. Open the Migration Object Modeler App

Launch the SAP Fiori app “Model Your Migration Objects” and enter your migration project.

When you open the app for the first time you get the information, that in this app it is only possible to view migration projects and objects, which use the approach "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables". To view migration projects and migration objects that use the migration approach "Migrate Data Directly from SAP system", use the "Implementation Activities" app to access the SAP S/4HANA migration object modeler.

2. Locate the Supplier Migration Object

Expand the migration objects node to view all objects. Double-click “Supplier” to open it.

 

3. Display Source Structures

Double-click “Source Structures.” If technical names aren’t visible, go to Settings and enable them.

4. Switch to Edit Mode

Change to Edit mode to allow modifications.

5. Add the New Field

Select the relevant source structure and click “Add Field.” Enter the details for your new field (e.g., ZZ_NEW_FIELD). The system flags it as a custom field. In our example we want to add a the field CONTACTALLOWANCECTRCENBP to the migration object Supplier.

6. Save Your Changes

Save your entries. Use the “Full Screen on/off” button to toggle the view if needed.

7. Map the New Field

Double-click “Field Mapping” (ensure you’re still in Edit mode). Search for your new field in the source structure and the corresponding target field (e.g., CONTACTALLOWANCECTRCENBP). Drag your new field to the target field, or right-click and choose “Assign Rule.”

8. Verify and Save the Mapping

Double-check that the mapping is correct and save your changes.

9. Synchronize Structures

Synchronize the structures to apply your changes. Confirm any pop-ups and exit the application.

10. Validate in SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit

Go back to “Migrate Your Data – migration cockpit.” Check your project and review the staging tables. If the table status is “Adjustment Required,” select the table and click “Adjust Structure.” If you have already done this step in the Model your Migration App, the status should be "OK".

11. Find the Field in the Template

The newly added field will now be visible in the XML template file for the migration object.

Additional Tips

  • Resetting Objects: You can reset a migration object to its original template version if needed. This removes all custom changes.
  • Updating Objects: SAP may update migration objects periodically. If prompted, update the object to ensure compatibility.
  • Favorites: Add frequently used projects or objects to your favorites for quick access (up to 10 each).

References

  • https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3019787
  • https://help.sap.com/viewer/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/latest/en-US/15a578b94cd24ff9bcd4317f9d745061.html
  • SAP S/4HANA Migration Object Modeler for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best regards,

Kirstin

entry.title: Issue – Purchase Requisition Creation for Lean Services in SAP PS
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are currently encountering an issue when generating Purchase Requisitions from components in SAP Project System for Lean Services. The scenario concerns materials of type SRV with Product Type Group 2 (Lean Services), within a project and network configured in Enhanced Procurement Mode. This mode has been correctly activated at both the Project Profile level and the Network/Plant level.

The first issue observed is the absence of any option related to services in the Project Builder user-specific settings. When navigating to Project Builder and accessing the user-specific options, only standard processable objects such as materials and activities are available. No setting allows services or lean services to be defined as processable objects.

The second issue occurs during the creation of a Purchase Requisition from a material component using Lean Service ( materials of type SRV with Product Type Group 2 ). When attempting to generate the PR, the system displays an error message requesting the entry of a “Validity Start Date.” However, this field is not available or visible in the Project Builder interface for either the component or the related network activity, making it impossible for the user to provide the required information.

This behavior is observed exclusively for Lean Services, as non stocked material components do not trigger the same error and function correctly.

Experts, could you please help identify the root cause of this issue and propose an appropriate solution?

entry.title: Re: Agrupamento de itens MRP na ordem via MD01
entry.content:
Olá @Dayanna_Paris ,
Não, o SAP MRP não permite agrupar vários materiais em uma única ordem planejada ou requisição.

A transação MD01 cria uma ordem planejada para cada necessidade de material.

A transação MD15 pode converter várias ordens planejadas de uma só vez, mas cada uma ainda se torna uma linha de requisição separada.

O agrupamento só ocorre posteriormente: você pode combinar requisições em uma única ordem de compra durante o processo de aquisição (por exemplo, ME21N/ME59N).

Portanto, a solução padrão mais próxima é a conversão coletiva com agrupamento na etapa da ordem de compra.

entry.title: Re: New GL: account assignment table
entry.content:
For your CDS view

If you’re still on ECC with New GL:

  • You’ll need to join FAGLFLEXA (GL line items) with COEP (CO line items) using common fields like document number, fiscal year, and line item.
  • That’s how you retrieve the CO object assignment for P&L accounts.

If you’re on S/4HANA 

  • Use ACDOCA directly. It already includes both FI and CO dimensions, so you don’t need to join separate tables.
  • Fields like KOSTL (cost center), AUFNR (order), PRCTR (profit center), etc. are available in ACDOCA.

entry.title: NF-e Mapping Error: J_1BNFE_CF013 - Invalid Character in Decimal Field
entry.content:
Hey guys,

I am facing a persistent mapping error when processing an NF-e from Brazil in an S/4HANA environment. XML generation stops and the following error message is displayed on the monitor:

"Error mapping the NF-e. The character - is not a decimal digit, a decimal point or the exponential notation "e"."

Situation analysis:

The J_1BNFE_CF013 error occurs when executing output mapping logic, specifically when the system attempts to move a string value containing a hyphen ( -) to a numeric or decimal target field. In our case, the XML is not even generated because the mapping process (probably within a J_1B_NF_MAP_TO_XML function or CL_NFE_PRINT class) fails during the data type conversion.

We check common fields such as Weights (NTGEW/ BRGEW), NCM and State Registration, but the "Character -" error persists even when these fields appear without errors in the ALV display.

Has anyone come across this error?

entry.title: Defaulting “GR Non-Valuated” from PRs to POs
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to seek advice regarding the handling of the “GR Non-Valuated” indicator from PRs to POs.

We would like the “GR Non-Valuated” indicator to be defaulted (ticked) in POs created from PRs with account assignment K (Cost Center).

However, we understand that this behavior cannot be changed via standard configuration, as we cannot change the standard account assignment setup. We have explored the option of creating a custom account assignment category based on K, but Goods Receipt cannot be posted for custom categories, as SAP only supports GR posting for standard account assignment categories (K, P, C, S, F, etc.), as documented in SAP KBA 3387525 (Error M7064)(https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3387525) 

May we kindly confirm whether there are any other supported configuration options to default the “GR Non-Valuated” indicator, or if account assignment categories are the only available approach?

Thank you very much for your guidance.

Thank you.

Best regards, 

HY

entry.title: LGM Knowledge Snippet: Processing Variant Maintenance
entry.content:
In my previous post about Processing Variants, I explained the concept and how they control shipping process behavior in SAP Logistics Management. Now let's dive into the practical side: how do you actually configure and maintain Processing Variants using the Configure Shipping Processes app?

The Configure Shipping Processes App

The Configure Shipping Processes app (find the complete documentation here) is your central tool for managing Processing Variants. This Fiori application provides a clear interface for viewing SAP-delivered variants and creating custom ones tailored to your business requirements.

Viewing Existing Processing Variants

When you open the Configure Shipping Processes app, you'll see a list of all available Processing Variants-both the standard ones delivered by SAP and any custom variants your organization has created.

The list view provides several useful capabilities:

  • Filter by Shipping Process - Quickly narrow down to Parcel, LTL, Direct LTL, FTL, or No Shipping variants
  • Search by Display ID or Description - Find specific variants quickly in large catalogs
  • See SAP-Managed indicator - Immediately identify which variants are delivered by SAP vs. customer-created
  • Check Default status - See which variant is marked as the default fallback
  • View creation and modification details - Showing when variants were created and last changed

SAP delivers five standard Processing Variants out of the box:

  • /SAP/Parcel - Parcel shipments with carrier integrations
  • /SAP/LTL - Consolidated Less-Than-Truckload shipments
  • /SAP/Direct_LTL - Direct LTL with individual carrier confirmation
  • /SAP/No_Shipping - Warehouse-only scenarios

These delivered variants are read-only and serve as tested, stable baselines. You cannot modify them, but you can create your own custom variants based on the same Shipping Processes.

Creating a Custom Processing Variant

When your business has specific requirements that the standard variants don't address, you can create custom Processing Variants. Here's the step-by-step workflow:

Step 1: Start Creation

Click the "Create" button in the Configure Shipping Processes app. The system opens a new draft variant in edit mode.

Step 2: Select the Base Shipping Process

Choose which fundamental Shipping Process your variant will be based on:

  • Parcel - If you need special parcel carrier handling
  • LTL - For consolidated shipments with regular carrier schedules
  • Direct LTL - For shipments requiring explicit carrier confirmation
  • No Shipping - For warehouse-internal processes

This choice is critical because it determines which configuration options will be available in the next steps. The Shipping Process defines the foundational behavior and which attributes you can customize.

Step 3: Define the Display ID

Create a unique Display ID for your variant. Use a clear naming convention that indicates the purpose, for example:

  • "CUSTOM_LTL_EXPRESS" - For expedited LTL shipments
  • "CUSTOM_LTL_CONSOLIDATION" - For LTL with consolidation cutoff enabled
  • "CUSTOM_DIRECTLTL_MANUAL_CONFIRM" - For Direct LTL requiring manual carrier approval

Avoid generic names like "VARIANT_001" or "CUSTOM_1"-your operations team (and maybe also the SAP development team ;-)) will thank you months later when reviewing configurations or troubleshooting.

Step 4: Add a Description

Provide a clear, detailed description explaining:

  • What business scenario this variant supports
  • How it differs from standard SAP variants
  • Any special handling or prerequisites
  • Which organizational units or locations should use it

Note: So far, the delivered processing variants come with translated descriptions, this is not yet available for customer defined processing variants. 

Step 5: Configure Available Options

The app displays configuration options based on the Shipping Process you selected. Not all options are available for all processes-the system only shows settings that are meaningful for your chosen process type.

Currently there is only one option you can maintain (the idea here is of course, to add more step by step):

Consolidation Cutoff (available for LTL and Direct LTL)

Enable this when you want to consolidate multiple Transportation Requests into fewer Consignments during a defined time window. As I explained in the previous Processing Variants post, this dramatically changes process behavior:

  • The system searches for existing open Consignments when creating new Transportation Requests
  • Multiple requests get grouped into the same Consignment
  • Carrier determination happens after the consolidation window closes
  • A Consignment without a carrier is acceptable during consolidation but requires attention afterward

More details about consolidation cutoff can be found in the SAP Help Portal documentation.

The following picture shows an example oif a manually created Processing Variant:

 

 Step 6: Save as Draft

While working on your variant, you can save it as a draft. Draft variants are fully editable-you can change any configuration option, the Display ID, descriptions, or even the base Shipping Process.

Draft variants are not yet available for use in Transportation Requests or Consignments. Use the draft phase to validate your configuration before activation.

Step 7: Activate the Variant

Once you're satisfied with the configuration, activate the variant. This makes it available for:

  • Business rules that determine Processing Variants automatically
  • Manual selection when creating Transportation Requests
  • Assignment to Transportation Requests through APIs

Critical: After activation, the configuration options become immutable. You can still update descriptions and translations, but you cannot change which features are enabled or the base Shipping Process. This immutability ensures that active Transportation Requests and Consignments cannot be affected by configuration changes mid-process.

The Golden Rules of Processing Variant Maintenance

To ensure system stability and maintain trust in your logistics processes, Processing Variants follow strict governance rules:

Rule 1: Configuration Immutability After Activation

Once a Processing Variant is activated and referenced by any Transportation Request or Consignment, its configuration attributes are permanently locked. You cannot:

  • Change which Shipping Process it's based on
  • Enable or disable consolidation cutoff
  • Modify handling unit requirements
  • Alter any other process-controlling attributes

Why this restriction? Imagine you have 50 active Consignments using a variant configured for consolidation. If you could suddenly disable consolidation, those Consignments would be in an undefined state—the system wouldn't know whether to continue consolidating or stop. The immutability rule prevents such scenarios.

If you need to change a variant's configuration, create a new variant with your desired settings. Update your business rules to use the new variant for future Transportation Requests.

What You CAN Change After Activation:

  • Descriptions - Improve wording, add clarifications, fix typos
  • Default status - Mark a different variant as default

These changes don't affect process behavior for existing documents, so they're safe to modify at any time.

We already have ideas for a deprecation process for processing variants, but this will w´be introduced in a later step.

Rule 2: SAP-Managed Variants Are Completely Read-Only

The Processing Variants delivered by SAP (identifiable by Display IDs starting with "/SAP/") are completely read-only. You cannot modify:

  • Configuration options
  • Descriptions (including translations)
  • Display IDs
  • Any other attributes

SAP maintains these variants as tested, stable baselines. When SAP needs to update descriptions or add new translations, these changes come through system updates and migrations.

If an SAP-delivered variant almost meets your needs but requires one change, create a custom variant instead. Copy the concept, adjust the configuration, and give it your own Display ID.

Rule 3: Only One Default Variant

You can designate exactly one Processing Variant as the default. This variant serves as a fallback when:

  • Business rules cannot determine a specific variant
  • APIs create Transportation Requests without specifying a variant
  • Manual processes need a "safe" default choice

When you mark a variant as default using the "Set Default Processing Variant" action, the system automatically removes the default flag from whichever variant previously held it. There's no risk of accidentally having multiple defaults.

Choosing the Right Default:

Select a default variant that represents your most common or most flexible scenario. Typically, this means:

  • A variant without highly restrictive settings
  • One that applies to the broadest set of shipments
  • Your "standard" process that covers 70-80% of cases

The /SAP/No_Shipping variant is delivered as the default, but you'll likely want to change this to your primary shipping process variant once you've configured your business rules.

Setting the Default Processing Variant

To set a different variant as the default:

  1. Navigate to the Configure Shipping Processes app
  2. Select the variant you want to make default
  3. Click the "Set Default Processing Variant" action in the action toolbar
  4. The system updates the selected variant's default status and clears it from the previous default

The change takes effect immediately and applies to all future Transportation Requests where no specific variant is determined.

Troubleshooting Common Maintenance Scenarios

Scenario 1: "I need to change a configuration option on an active variant"

Solution: You cannot modify active variant configurations. Instead:

  1. Create a new custom variant with the desired configuration
  2. Give it a new Display ID (e.g., add "_V2" to indicate version 2)
  3. Update your business rules to use the new variant for future Transportation Requests
  4. Existing Transportation Requests continue using the old variant until completion
  5. Once all Transportation Requests using the old variant are complete, you can consider deprecating it

Scenario 2: "Which variant should I choose as the default?"

Solution: Analyze your Transportation Request creation patterns:

  • Which Shipping Process do you use most frequently?
  • What percentage of Transportation Requests can't be determined by business rules?
  • Which variant represents your "safest" fallback process?

For most customers, the default should be either /SAP/LTL or /SAP/Direct_LTL, depending on whether you typically consolidate shipments or handle them individually.

Scenario 3: "How do I know which variant a Transportation Request is using?"

Solution: The Processing Variant is displayed in the Transportation Request details. You can also filter Transportation Requests by Processing Variant in the Manage Transportation Requests app.

If you need to analyze variant usage at scale, the Processing Variant field is available in reporting and analytics.

Scenario 4: "Can I delete a Processing Variant that's no longer needed?"

Solution: Deletion of Processing Variants is not currently supported through the UI. Here's why:

  • Historical Transportation Requests reference the variant
  • Reporting and analytics need the variant definition
  • Audit trails require variant information

Instead of deletion, consider creating clear naming conventions that indicate deprecated variants (e.g., prefixing with "DEPRECATED_") and updating descriptions to indicate they should no longer be used.

Integration with Business Rules

While the Configure Shipping Processes app lets you create and maintain Processing Variants, the real power comes from integrating them with business rules for automatic determination.

In the Business Rules configuration, you can define rules like:

  • "For Transportation Requests from Shipping Point 1000 with weight < 500kg → use CUSTOM_LTL_CONSOLIDATION"
  • "For express orders → use CUSTOM_LTL_EXPRESS"

The Processing Variants you create in the Configure Shipping Processes app become available as targets in these business rules. This separation of concerns is powerful:

  • Processing Variants define what each process variation does
  • Business Rules define when each variant should be used

You can change business rules (when to use a variant) without affecting variant configurations (what the variant does).

Wrapping Up

The Configure Shipping Processes app puts powerful configuration capabilities at your fingertips, letting you tailor SAP Logistics Management to your exact business requirements without coding. By understanding the maintenance workflows, governance rules, and best practices, you can confidently create and manage Processing Variants that support your logistics operations.

Key takeaways for maintaining Processing Variants:

  • Start with SAP-delivered variants and only create custom ones when truly needed
  • Use clear, descriptive Display IDs and comprehensive descriptions
  • Remember that configurations are immutable after activation—test thoroughly first
  • Integrate with business rules for automatic, intelligent variant determination
  • Maintain documentation and review periodically to prevent configuration drift

Have you created custom Processing Variants in your LGM implementation? What configuration options would you like to see added in future releases? Share your experiences and suggestions in the comments!

entry.title: The New Geomap Control in MDC Library: Bring SAP Geomap Into Your UI5 Apps
entry.content:
Intro
We’re excited to announce the release of an experimental MDC control for displaying interactive maps — visualizing GeoJSON data, integrating vector and raster tiles from various providers, enabling features like clustering and selection, or handling user interaction — sap.ui.mdc.Geomap offers a native UI5 interface to achieve this without building custom wrappers.  

 

 

What’s in it for you  

  • Faster integration: Directly embed a production-grade map component into your UI5 applications, leveraging familiar UI5 properties, events, and aggregations.  
  • Flexible data & visualization: Seamlessly combine various tile providers (ESRI, MapBox, Azure maps, SAP HSS, etc.) with GeoJSON data sources. Manage diverse layers like fill, line, circle, heatmap, or symbol layers directly from your application’s model.  
  • Native UI5 behavior: Benefit from standard UI5 control lifecycle, data binding, and composition, ensuring the map integrates smoothly with your existing UI5 patterns.  
  • Build-friendly: This library is specifically designed for UI5 runtime consumption.  
  • SAP HANA Spatial Services (HSS) integration: Allows to run with data provided from HSS.

 

What’s included  

  • A new experimental control in MDC library:  sap.ui.mdc.Geomap  
  • Core building blocks: The main Geomap control, alongside specialized controls for providers, data sources, various layer types (such as, GeomapFillLayer, GeomapLineLayer, GeomapHeatmapLayer, GeomapSymbolLayer), and common map UI controls (such as, GeomapNavigationControl, GeomapScaleControl, GeomapSelectionControl, GeomapCopyrightControl).  
  • Full capabilities including: Rendering a dynamic, responsive map canvas with pan, zoom, rotate, and tilt capabilities. Supports smooth animations, full-screen mode, and adapts to all screen sizes, including mobile devices.  Built on WebGL for high-performance rendering. Map view can be programmatically controlled or manipulated by the user .

 

Multi-provider support  

Integrates with multiple map data providers for maximum flexibility. Out-of-the-box support for ESRI, HERE, OpenStreetMap and SAP HANA Spatial Services. Easily switch providers or configure custom styles and tokens.  

 

Rich layer system  

Visualize geospatial data using multiple layer types, each optimized for different visualization needs.  

  • Fill Layer: Colored polygons for regions, zones, or districts.  
  • Circle Layer: Point markers with customizable radius and color.  
  • Line Layer: Draw paths, routes, or connections.  
  • Heatmap Layer: Visualize data density or clustering.  
  • Symbol Layer: Display icons and text labels for feature identification.  

 

Advanced clustering  

  • Automatically groups nearby features to reduce clutter and improve performance.  
  • Dynamic cluster sizing, customizable properties, and click-through expansion. Especially useful for large datasets.  

  

Drawing & selection tools  

Enables users to select or annotate map features using lasso, circle, polygon or rectangle tools. Supports multi-feature selection.   

 

GeoJSON data support  

  •  Loads and visualizes standard GeoJSON data, including points, lines, polygons, and multi-geometries.  
  • Supports dynamic updates, feature properties, and optimized rendering for large datasets. Clustering available for point data.  

  

Comprehensive controls  

  •  Built-in UI controls for navigation, scale, fullscreen, copyright, and selection. 
  •  Controls can be positioned, enabled/disabled. 

  

Event-driven architecture  

Rich event system for application integration and interactive workflows. Listens for map lifecycle, user interaction, feature, drawing, and data update events.  

 

Styling & theming  

Professional, customizable styling with support for multiple color palettes, icons, and themes.  Data-driven styling rules, CSS variable integration, and dark/light theme support.  

 

Performance optimization  

Designed for enterprise-scale performance. Automatic tile-based rendering, lazy loading, efficient clustering, and WebGL acceleration.   

 

Internationalization  

Inclusive design with keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, and multi-language support. ARIA labels, high contrast mode, and full i18n support.  

   

How to use it  

The sap.ui.mdc.Geomap library is delivered as part of OpenUI5. It’s designed to be included and consumed through your standard UI5 build processes or runtime distribution.  

Link to samples: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/entity/sap.ui.mdc.Geomap

MDC tutorial: https://github.com/SAP-samples/ui5-mdc-json-tutorial/tree/main/u2/ex1

 

Get started  

As an experimental library, OpenUI5 Geomap is in its initial stages, and its API is not yet finalized. This presents a unique opportunity for you to contribute to its evolution by providing feedback and suggestions. Be aware that some APIs may change or be removed in future updates.   

We are excited to see the innovative applications you will create using the SAPUI5 Geomap library.   

Happy geocoding!

entry.title: Re: U.S SECURE 2.0 Act - Section 603
entry.content:
Hello @James297009,

Thank you for reaching out. As you mentioned, the creation of /TB6 is essential to trigger the Section 603 rollover option. If /TB6 was not generated this is the root cause of your issue. Below are the responses to your questions:

  • Did you have to trigger /TB6 manually somehow? No, the /TB6 wage type is automatically generated in the first payroll period of the year.

  • Did you have to run a retro into December? This is not necessary. The system will read TCRT values of the prior year to create /TB6, even if a retro isn’t performed.

  • Did payroll only pick up /TB6 when the prior year’s results existed in TCRT? Yes, that is correct. To create /TB6, the values of wage type /703 must exist in the TCRT table of the prior year. If no prior year payroll results are present, you can generate /TB6 via /TB7. However, for this scenario, it would be necessary to exceed the 150k threshold in /TB7 to trigger the rollover.

  • Were there additional steps needed beyond what the note describes? All necessary steps are detailed in the Notes.

If you have followed the necessary steps and the rollover is still not occurring, please open a case with PA-PA-US-BN so that we can review the situation.

entry.title: Congratulatory leave quota entitlement for different dependents
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

Congratulatory leave if utilized for the first time, employee cannot apply for second occasion under this leave due to quota provided for one time as per calendar year basis.

So basically this is what we are trying to achieve. 

  1. If an employee has two children, they will be entitled to 4 working days (WD) of Congratulatory Leave (Marriage of Child), with 2 WD per child and be able to apply for it within the same year. 
  2. The child must be single. If the child is divorced, the employee will not be entitled to the quota for that child. 
  3. If the employee has fully utilized the quota and all children are married, no additional quota will be generated. 
  4. This applies only to legal (biological) children and is not applicable to other types of dependents. YES
  5. The quota for the following year will be generated based on the child’s marital status (Single).

Example: If an employee has 3 children and 2 of them got married in 2025, the quota for 2026 will be 2 WD, as only one child remains unmarried. 

I have updated the rules, but didnt work. Any idea to update the rules?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Following messages raised while executing migration o...
entry.content:
Hello @Fabiane_Melo74

Yes, there is a way but that will depend on what was posted.

To specifically answer your questions:

  • Is there a solution for me to reload the template with the correct values and have it account for them?
    • Yes, but typically via new project/new run, not by overwriting the finalised posted run.
  • Do I have to open another migration project in order to be able to execute the template?
    • In practice, yes for final-stage successful posted migrations.

Here are some details to consume:

  • If posting migration object is already completed and documents were posted in final migration mode, then you generally cannot re-simulate and overwrite the same posted run-in place.
  • For you to load corrected values, I suggest you create a new migration project or a new object run, reload the corrected file and post the correction properly.

Something you should consider before making your decision:

  1. If you had already posted wrong value to accounting, you need business correction postings and then load the corrected values as you may require. Do not try to replace already posted migration documents directly.
  2. If your system did not record any accounting impact despite objects being technically completed, you may use a new migration project for clean reprocessing.

Best regards

Chris
